name: Laboratory tests review
pageTitle: Laboratory Tests To Be Performed - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057 - V1
heading: Laboratory tests
secondaryActions: .MACROLIDES (row test-name link — edits/opens that recorded test)
flowContext: This page belongs to the BCP/inspector DECISION flow (URL prefix /decision/...), not the notifier's pre-notification journey. It is the review/list endpoint of a multi-page lab-test sub-flow reached from the decision hub 'Laboratory tests' link (ched-d-workflows.ts:410). The data-entry happens on prior pages: Lab tests intro (Yes/No/Random) -> [Reason (Suspicion) — CHED-P only] -> Commodity select -> Commodity test search -> Commodity sample -> Record laboratory test information. Confirmed by the QA page-object set under page-objects/decision/ (LabTestsIntroPage, LabTestsReasonPage, LabTestsCommoditySelectPage, LabTestsCommodityTestPage, LabTestsCommoditySamplePage, RecordLaboratoryTestInformationPage, LabTestsReviewPage) and the CHED-D workflow at ched-d-workflows.ts:410-449. LEGACY: the IPAFFS template for this page is ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/bip/labTestsToBePerformed.html and its route handler is ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/routes/handlers/bip/lab_tests/lab_tests_to_be_performed.js — this is a decision-frontend (BIP) surface, NOT the notifier frontend (ipaffs-frontend-notification), so the notifier ValidationMessages.properties and PartOne validation groups do not govern it.

structure.govukComponent: Heading xl (span#page-primary-title inside the govuk-heading-xl). LEGACY: rendered by the pageTitleWithReferenceNumber partial with pageTitle="Laboratory tests" (labTestsToBePerformed.html:19).
Heading m (wrapped in a govuk-form-group div). LEGACY: labTestsToBePerformed.html:21-23.

structure.text: Columns: Test | Commodity code | Description | Species | Analysis type | Result | Remove (last header label visually hidden). One recorded row observed: .MACROLIDES | 10064000 | Broken rice | (empty) | Initial | Satisfactory | Remove. LEGACY: column data sources are testName, commodityCode, commodityDescription, speciesName, applicant.analysisType, laboratoryTestResult.conclusion (labTestsToBePerformed.html:41-56). commodityDescription is derived server-side by matching partOne.commodities.commodityComplement by commodityID (handler :82-84); Result conclusion defaults to 'Pending' when unset (handler :79-81).
structure.govukComponent: Table (custom id=LabTestsTable, extra class lab-test-table, aria-label "Lab tests table")

structure.text: LEGACY: when there are no recorded lab tests the table body renders a single row 'There are no laboratory tests to be performed' (colspan=5). No CHED-D trace exercised the empty state — both observed traces had exactly one row. Confirms the review/list surface is reachable with zero tests (it does not redirect away), which bears on the GATING open question.

structure.govukComponent: None — bespoke class link-button (submit button styled as a link, name=add-labTest value=add). LEGACY: routes to the Lab test choose-commodity page (LAB_TEST_CHOOSE_COMMODITY_PATH) when add-labTest is in the payload (handler :42-43, 61-63).
Button (govuk-button govuk-!-margin-right-1, name=save-button value="Save and return"). LEGACY: id=button-save-and-return, data-prevent-double-click=true (labTestsToBePerformed.html:72-79).

Button (govuk-button govuk-!-margin-right-1, name=save-button value="Save and continue"). LEGACY: id=button-save-and-continue, data-prevent-double-click=true. This button is rendered ONLY when editTestsOnly is false; when the notification status is VALIDATED or REJECTED (editTestsOnly true) it is REPLACED by a hidden input return-to-certificate-overview=true (labTestsToBePerformed.html:80-91, handler :91-92).

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 — contains a nested reference-number/version span (heading-tertiary) plus the primary title span 'Laboratory tests'

govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:22

govukComponents.usage: Lists the laboratory tests recorded against the notification (one row per test): Test, Commodity code, Description, Species, Analysis type, Result, plus a Remove action column
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151; LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:24-65

govukComponents.usage: Wraps the 'Review' H2 (used purely as a spacing container here, no field inside)
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:21-23

govukComponents.usage: Two primary submit buttons in one row: 'Save and return' and 'Save and continue' (both share name=save-button, disambiguated by value). data-prevent-double-click=true on both. Both are named controls on the page object (LabTestsReviewPage.ts:10 btnSaveAndReturn, :14 btnSaveAndContinue). 'Save and continue' is a genuinely-used path — driven twice on the review page in the CHED-P decision workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:1056-1057) — though in the CHED-D workflow only 'Save and return' is clicked (ched-d-workflows.ts:449). LEGACY: 'Save and continue' is conditional — hidden and replaced by a return-to-certificate-overview hidden input when editTestsOnly (status VALIDATED/REJECTED) is true, so the count is 2 only in the in-progress state observed by the traces (labTestsToBePerformed.html:80-91).
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; page object page-objects/decision/LabTestsReviewPage.ts:10,14; CHED-P usage workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1056-1057; LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:71-92, handler lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:91-92

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A submit <button> styled to look like a text link. Used for three in-table/row actions: the test-name cell (name=testResult value=<index>, opens/edits that recorded test), the Remove cell (name=remove-labTest value=<index>), and the 'Add another test' control below the table (name=add-labTest value=add). Each posts back to the same form to mutate the lab-test list server-side. LEGACY: the handler routes on payload key — remove-labTest re-renders the list, add-labTest goes to choose-commodity, testResult goes to the Record lab test results page for that index (handler :38-51). The test-name row link is the only one of the three the tests actually drive: LabTestsReviewPage.ts:6 exposes btnLabTest(name) and it is clicked in the CHED-P workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:1116) to re-open a recorded test — corroborating that the row test-name link opens/edits that test. 'Add another test' and 'Remove' have NO page-object counterpart, so they were never test-driven (trace-render + legacy-template evidence only).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a GOV.UK Design System class. IPAFFS renders link-styled actions as submit buttons so each mutation is a POST. In the new app these should be modelled explicitly: 'Add another test' as a standard action, and per-row 'Change'/'Remove' actions. The row-level pattern maps cleanly to a GOV.UK Summary list with actions or a Table with an actions column using govuk-link.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-summary-list (with __actions Change/Remove links) for the recorded-tests list, OR govuk-table + govuk-link action cells; 'Add another test' as a secondary govuk-button or govuk-link
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146 (main HTML: three button.link-button elements); LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:41-70; handler lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:38-67; row test-name link corroborated page-objects/decision/LabTestsReviewPage.ts:6 + workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1116

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke span inside the govuk-heading-xl H1 that carries the notification reference number (#reference-number) and version (#cved-version-number) above the primary page title (#page-primary-title). Acts as a visual caption but is not built with the Design System caption pattern.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IPAFFS uses a custom in-heading caption instead of the standard govuk-caption-xl span. Semantically the reference/version is caption-like context for the page title.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-caption-xl span before the H1 text (standard 'section caption' pattern)
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146 (H1 innerHTML); LEGACY pageTitleWithReferenceNumber partial invoked at labTestsToBePerformed.html:19

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The govuk-table carries an extra bespoke class lab-test-table and a custom id, presumably for IPAFFS-specific CSS/JS hooks.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Low concern — it is still a standard govuk-table underneath. The extra class is an app-specific styling hook the new app would not need if it uses the standard component.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Plain govuk-table (or govuk-summary-list) with no bespoke class

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Visually hides the 'Remove' text in the final table header cell while keeping it available to assistive tech.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IPAFFS uses a bespoke .hidden utility rather than the Design System visually-hidden class.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146 (Remove); LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:33

fields.name: testResult (row link)
fields.label: Test
fields.hint: Table column 1 — the reagent/analyte-group name, rendered as a link-button (name=testResult value=<index>) that re-opens/edits that recorded test. LEGACY: bound to {{testName}} of each labTest (labTestsToBePerformed.html:41-44); clicking it (payload key testResult) routes to the Record lab test results page for that index (handler :44-45, RECORD_LAB_TEST_RESULTS_PATH). Chosen upstream by searching on the Lab tests commodity-test page (ched-d-workflows.ts:420-422). The row link is exercised by the tests: page object btnLabTest(name) re-opens a recorded test in CHED-P (ched-p-workflows.ts:1116). Observed value: .MACROLIDES. Domain vocabulary (lab-test.ts:1-7) also includes: .ANTITHYROID AGENTS, .CARBAMATES, .PHENICOLES, .AMINOGLYCOSIDE/AMINOSIDE.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151. Row-link edit behaviour + template binding from LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:41-44 + handler lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:44-45, page-objects/decision/LabTestsReviewPage.ts:6, workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1116 and types/lab-test.ts:1-7 (the 4 non-observed options are inferred, not seen in a CHED-D trace).

fields.name: commodity-code (display)
fields.label: Commodity code
fields.hint: Table column 2 — read-only display of the commodity code the test applies to (bound to {{commodityCode}} — labTestsToBePerformed.html:46). Observed value: 10064000
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151; LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:46

fields.name: description (display)
fields.label: Description
fields.hint: Table column 3 — read-only commodity description. LEGACY: derived server-side, not stored on the lab test — the handler looks up partOne.commodities.commodityComplement whose commodityID matches the test's commodityCode and reads its commodityDescription (defaulting to '' if not found), then binds {{commodityDescription}} (handler :82-84, labTestsToBePerformed.html:47). Observed value: Broken rice
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151; LEGACY lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:82-84 + labTestsToBePerformed.html:47

fields.name: species (display)
fields.label: Species
fields.hint: Table column 4 — read-only species (bound to {{speciesName}} — labTestsToBePerformed.html:48). Empty in both observed CHED-D rows. HRFNAO (CHED-D) commodities are of non-animal origin, so Species is expected to be empty for CHED-D; the QA suite only drives Species selection for CHED-A/CHED-P (commodityBasicDescription.checkboxSpecies — ched-a-workflows.ts:277, ched-p-workflows.ts:298), never in the CHED-D workflow.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146 (empty cell); corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151 (empty cell); LEGACY labTestsToBePerformed.html:48; CHED-D workflow drives no Species (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts has no checkboxSpecies call)

fields.name: analysis-type (display)
fields.label: Analysis type
fields.hint: Table column 5 — read-only analysis type (bound to {{applicant.analysisType}} — labTestsToBePerformed.html:49). LEGACY resolves the earlier gap: the handler maps a stored applicant.analysisType through analysisTypeMap before display — 'Initial analysis'->'Initial', 'Counter analysis'->'Counter', 'Second expert analysis'->'Second expert' (handler :26-30, :88). So the full displayed value set is Initial / Counter / Second expert. Observed value: Initial.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151. Full value set (Initial/Counter/Second expert) is LEGACY from the analysisTypeMap in lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:26-30,88; only 'Initial' was observed in a trace.

fields.name: result (display)
fields.label: Result
fields.hint: Table column 6 — read-only test result (bound to {{laboratoryTestResult.conclusion}} — labTestsToBePerformed.html:50). LEGACY: entered upstream on the Record lab test results page as a radio with three options Satisfactory / Not satisfactory / Not interpretable (labTestResults.html:223-238); when no result has been recorded yet the handler defaults the displayed conclusion to 'Pending' (handler :79-81). So the full displayed value set is Pending (default) / Satisfactory / Not satisfactory / Not interpretable. Observed value: Satisfactory.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 146; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 151. 'Not satisfactory' + 'Not interpretable' options and 'Pending' default are LEGACY from labTestResults.html:223-238 and lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:79-81 (only 'Satisfactory' seen in a CHED-D trace).

validationMessagesNote: LEGACY: this review/list page has NO server-side validation of its own — its route handler POST is a no-op (lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:99-101) and there is no validation schema wired to this path. The nearby laboratory_tests_validation.js (Joi, name field max 255 with a max-length message) governs the upstream lab-test name/search field, NOT this review page. So validationMessages is correctly empty; the earlier 'error copy is a gap' open question is resolved to 'no page-level validation exists' rather than 'unknown'.
openQuestions: This is a read-only review/list page: the six columns (Test, Commodity code, Description, Species, Analysis type, Result) are displayed here, not entered here. The actual data entry happens on the per-test detail pages upstream (Lab tests commodity-test search, Lab tests commodity-sample, Record laboratory test information). The 'fields' recorded here are the displayed columns, transcribed verbatim from the table headers/cells — they are display columns, not inputs on this page. LEGACY confirms this: the template only binds each column to a lab-test property and the POST handler is a no-op (labTestsToBePerformed.html:37-63, handler :99-101).

FLOW CONTEXT (corroborated by QA repo + LEGACY handler routing): This page is part of the BCP/inspector DECISION flow (URL /decision/...), not the notifier pre-notification journey. The sub-flow that populates it is: decision hub 'Laboratory tests' link -> Lab tests intro (Yes/No/Random) -> [Reason (Suspicion) — CHED-P only] -> Commodity select -> Commodity test search -> Commodity sample (Laboratory, Sample reference number, Number of samples, Sample type, Storage temperature) -> Record laboratory test information (Sample use-by date, Released date, Laboratory test method, Results, Satisfactory/Not satisfactory) -> THIS review page. See ched-d-workflows.ts:410-449 and the page-objects/decision/LabTests* set. LEGACY: the add-labTest button routes to LAB_TEST_CHOOSE_COMMODITY_PATH and testResult routes to RECORD_LAB_TEST_RESULTS_PATH (handler :42-45).

GATING: On the Lab tests intro page choosing 'No' (radioNo) records no lab test and this review page is skipped — the CHED-D 'permit'/'reject' workflows go intro(No) -> straight to decision scope (ched-d-workflows.ts:483-486, 506-509). LEGACY nuance: the template itself does NOT redirect when the list is empty — it renders an empty-table row 'There are no laboratory tests to be performed' (labTestsToBePerformed.html:59-62). So the review surface is reachable with zero tests; whether the new app should show an empty state or skip the page is a design choice, not an IPAFFS constraint.

The Species column was EMPTY in both observed CHED-D rows and the CHED-D workflow drives no Species selection. HRFNAO commodities are non-animal-origin, so Species is expected to be permanently empty for CHED-D — confirm whether the column should exist at all on the CHED-D surface. (The IPAFFS template renders the Species column unconditionally for all CHED types — labTestsToBePerformed.html:30,48.)

Only one lab-test row (.MACROLIDES / Broken rice) was observed in both CHED-D traces. LEGACY confirms multi-row rendering (the table {{#each labTestsToBePerformed}} loops all recorded tests — labTestsToBePerformed.html:38-58) and the empty-table state copy. The exact server targets are now LEGACY-known: add-labTest -> choose-commodity, testResult -> record-results(index), remove-labTest -> re-render list (handler :38-51). The test-name row link is also corroborated by the page object (btnLabTest, LabTestsReviewPage.ts:6) and CHED-P usage (ched-p-workflows.ts:1116).

'Save and continue' is a real, exercised path (page object LabTestsReviewPage.ts:14; driven twice in the CHED-P decision workflow ched-p-workflows.ts:1056-1057) but its destination was NOT exercised in the CHED-D traces. LEGACY resolves the routing: with no add/remove/testResult/return-to-certificate-overview key in the payload, the save button falls through to DECISION_PATH — the decision hub (handler :46-49). In the CHED-D workflow only 'Save and return' is clicked (ched-d-workflows.ts:449), returning to the decision hub (observed post-click URL .../CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057/hub) — consistent. NEW LEGACY finding: when notification status is VALIDATED or REJECTED (editTestsOnly), 'Save and continue' is hidden and 'Save and return' instead posts return-to-certificate-overview=true, routing to the certificate overview (CHED_OVERVIEW_PATH) rather than the decision hub (labTestsToBePerformed.html:80-91, handler :46-47,53-55,91-92).

The 'Analysis type' column showed 'Initial' in both CHED-D traces. RESOLVED by LEGACY: the displayed value comes from analysisTypeMap in the handler — the full set is Initial / Counter / Second expert (from stored 'Initial analysis' / 'Counter analysis' / 'Second expert analysis' — lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:26-30,88). CHED-A drives 'Random' and CHED-P drives 'Suspicion' upstream, but those are intro/reason selections, not this column's values.

No validation error state was captured — both evidence traces are happy-path (no govuk-error-summary present) and no CHED-D test asserts an error message on this page. RESOLVED by LEGACY: this page has no server-side validation of its own (no-op POST handler, lab_tests_to_be_performed.js:99-101), so there is no page-level error copy to capture — the review surface has no validation, rather than validation copy being merely unobserved.

The page is titled 'Laboratory Tests To Be Performed' but the H1 primary title is 'Laboratory tests' and the URL slug is 'lab-tests-to-be-performed' — three slightly different names for the same surface. LEGACY confirms this is intentional: the browser <title> constant is LAB_TESTS_TO_PERFORM_TITLE='Laboratory Tests To Be Performed' (constants.js:204) while the in-page H1 is pageTitle="Laboratory tests" (labTestsToBePerformed.html:19).

The QA LabTestsReviewPage page object exposes only three controls (btnLabTest(name), btnSaveAndReturn, btnSaveAndContinue) and no CHED-D test asserts on the table contents — so this corroboration confirms the interactive surface but adds no new asserted validation rule. The six display columns and their values remain trace-confirmed; their full value sets and data sources are now LEGACY-backed (see the analysis-type and result fields).
